subject: UX & Research
title: The Dark Pattern Subscription Trap
description: Learn The Dark Pattern Subscription Trap in one minute.

hook.text: Sign up in one click. Cancel? Navigate five pages, call a phone number, and wait on hold for 45 minutes.

buildup.text: The FTC calls these 'dark patterns': design tricks that manipulate users into things they didn't intend.

discovery.text: Amazon's Prime cancellation used to have twelve steps. The FTC sued, calling it an 'obstacle course.'

twist.text: California and the EU passed laws requiring cancellation to be as easy as sign-up. One-click cancel became law.

climax.text: Dark patterns trade short-term retention for long-term trust damage and now legal penalties.

punchline.text: If canceling is harder than signing up, you've lost the user's trust.

quiz.question: What did the FTC call Amazon's Prime cancellation process?
options: An obstacle course designed to prevent users from canceling
A security measure to prevent fraud
